title: Unguarded division — System.DivideByZeroException risk
description: Using a division operator (/) where the denominator may be zero without a preceding guard check. In PowerShell 5.1 and .NET, integer and floating-point division by zero both throw System.DivideByZeroException at runtime, which is untrapped and terminates the enclosing scope. Common occurrences: percentage calculations ($idx / $total), progress estimation ($elapsed / $estimated), ratio calculations, speed metrics. The exception is not surfaced until runtime with a real zero denominator, so unit tests that pass with sample data frequently miss it.
detection_method: Regex scan for division expressions whose left-hand denominator variable lacks a preceding -gt 0 guard on the same or prior line
exception_message: System.DivideByZeroException: Attempted to divide by zero.
preventionRule:
Always guard division with a non-zero check before the operation:
  if ($denominator -gt 0) { $ratio = $numerator / $denominator } else { $ratio = 0 }
For floating-point denominators use [Math]::Abs($denominator) -gt [double]::Epsilon.
Never use a bare ($a / $b) where $b originates from user input, array .Count, list length, elapsed time, or any value that can legitimately reach zero.
remedy:
Wrap division with a guard:
  # BEFORE (SIN)
  $pct = $done / $total

  # AFTER (safe)
  $pct = if ($total -gt 0) { $done / $total } else { 0 }

For repeated patterns, create a helper:
  function Invoke-SafeDivide { param([double]$n,[double]$d,[double]$fallback=0) if ($d -ne 0) { $n/$d } else { $fallback } }
trusted_fix_notes: Fix pattern applied successfully and tested in 2 confirmed cases (Invoke-ChecklistActions.ps1 + Script1.ps1). Pattern promoted to trusted after 2/4 required uses — still accumulating trust.
